agent.runner_summary: The simulator keeps chat history in memory and passes each new raw string user message through the project's existing streamed agent helper.

components.when_to_use: Use for isolated booking-lookup regressions where confirmation-code handling or booking summaries are wrong.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"confirmation_code": ...}) with the exact confirmation code observed in the log.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate whether the returned string correctly reports either the booking summary or the missing-booking message.

components.when_to_use: Use for isolated baggage-policy regressions when the issue is the policy text rather than the surrounding chat response.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"ticket_type": ...}) with the ticket type phrase from the scenario, such as "standard" or "basic economy".
components.output_guidance: Evaluate whether the returned string states the correct baggage allowance for the requested ticket type.

log_notes: Agent environments pass FixedTurn.content directly to the adapter as a raw string user message.
The adapter preserves prior user and assistant turns in order across the run.
OpenAI Agents SDK tool component targets should use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={...}) with named arguments only.
learning_env_guidance: Use plain string FixedTurn.content values that match a single chat user message; do not wrap agent inputs in JSON for this project.
Use FixedInput when exact wording matters, and PersonaInput when user persistence, confusion, or tone matters more than one exact sentence.
Use LLMJudgeEvaluator for conversational quality and policy-following checks; use CodeEvaluator for deterministic transcript, tool-call, or exact-output checks.
Prefer component targets for isolated regressions in lookup_booking or baggage_policy instead of a full agent run.
Use an agent target for seat-change scenarios, and mock change_seat when you need deterministic outcomes because the live tool mutates shared demo booking state.
When a generated environment needs ModelSpec, prefer provider="openai" so evaluator and persona calls can reuse OPENAI_API_KEY.
